claude.message: fix: Capture Claude: lines in User/Action response cards

added: if (boldOnly && (inExCard || inUaCard || (i + 3 < lines.length && lines.slice(i+1, i+4).some(function(l) { return l.trim().match(/^`<(example_user_memories|user)|^(User|Action|Claude):/); })))) {
removed: if (boldOnly && (inExCard || inUaCard || (i + 3 < lines.length && lines.slice(i+1, i+4).some(function(l) { return l.trim().match(/^`<(example_user_memories|user)|^(User|Action):/); })))) {

claude.summary: This change updates the system to recognize when a line starts with "Claude:" (in addition to "User:" or "Action:"). It does this by tweaking a regular expression to include "Claude" as a possible match, making the system more aware of CLAUDE's presence in the text.

claude.message: feat: Redesign claude.html with Anthropic docs aesthetic
claude.summary: This change updated the styling of CLAUDE's system prompt, giving it a fresh new look that matches Anthropic's aesthetic. The update tweaked things like font sizes, colors, and spacing to make the interface more visually appealing and user-friendly. Overall, it's a cosmetic change that improves the overall look and feel of the system prompt.

claude.message: feat: Add favicon to Claude System Prompt Explorer
claude.summary: This change just adds a tiny icon (called a favicon) to the website's tab in your browser, making it easier to spot. It doesn't change how the site works, just how it looks. The icon is stored in a file called "favicon.ico".

added: The user's name is GOOGLE_ACCOUNT_NAME , and their email address is HANDLE@gmail.com.
removed: The user's name is Ásgeir Thor Johnson, and their email address is asgeirtj@gmail.com.

gemini.summary: Someone updated the system prompt to change the user's name and email address. Now, instead of saying the user's name is Ásgeir Thor Johnson, it says their name is GOOGLE_ACCOUNT_NAME, and their email is HANDLE@gmail.com instead of asgeirtj@gmail.com. This change is likely to make the system more generic and flexible.

grok.summary: GROK's system prompt got an update that changed how it defines two search actions: "web_search" and "x_keyword_search". The "web_search" action now has a more detailed description, but its functionality remains the same. The "x_keyword_search" action remains unchanged, but its definition was reorganized in the update.
